critique: This role at GitLab clearly describes real AI engineering work, focusing on "embedding LLM-powered solutions into internal Sales, Marketing, and Support workflows" and "building agents with Agno and n8n" using Python and specific APIs. Despite the leadlens classifier noting 'no comp', the description provides strong technical content and a clear scope of work, indicating a genuine engineering position rather than an ai_washed scam.

The job description explicitly details a real AI engineering role, outlining responsibilities like "designs and deploys LLM-powered workflows, RAG pipelines, OCR, and agentic systems" and listing a comprehensive technical stack including "Python, LangChain, LangGraph, FastAPI." There are no indications of partnership opportunities, equity-only compensation, or vague descriptions typical of an ai_washed scam.

This role describes a clear technical product: "reimagining enterprise IT-services delivery with AI agents plus humans to build, integrate, and run the enterprise stack," and specific engineering responsibilities like "Owns architecture across agentic systems and full-stack product." It also lists a comprehensive technical stack, indicating a real engineering role, even if compensation isn't detailed.

The job description clearly outlines a real AI engineering role, specifying tasks like "Designs and builds agent-to-agent (A2A) systems" and utilizing technologies such as "OpenAI API and open-source LLMs, plus RAG and prompt engineering." Although the company is undisclosed and compensation is not mentioned, these are general posting issues and do not negate the presence of substantial engineering content, indicating it is not an "ai_washed" scam.

The description clearly outlines a senior ML engineering role focused on "scalable RAG systems and agentic architectures," with specific technologies like "vLLM/SGLang" and "LLM inference optimization." It describes concrete production-level work in GenAI, indicating a genuine engineering position. There are no scam signals like "partnership opportunity" or "no salary."

This job description details concrete AI engineering tasks, explicitly mentioning technologies like LangChain, FastAPI, Whisper, Neo4j, ChromaDB, and various cloud platforms. Despite the undisclosed company and lack of compensation details, it clearly outlines real technical work, fulfilling the criteria of a genuine engineering role.

This job description outlines a clear engineering role focused on "agentic systems" and "LLM tool-use in production, using n8n and the OpenAI API". Despite the leadlens flag for 'no comp', the description itself does not state "no salary" or frame it as a "partnership opportunity"; it's a standard SDE II position with real technical content.

This job description outlines a clear technical role for an 'Applied AI Engineer' focused on 'building agentic systems' and 'productionizing retrieval and tool-use' with specific technologies like 'Gemini, OpenAI API, agentic orchestration, Python'. Despite the leadlens flag for 'no comp', the detailed engineering tasks and product focus indicate a legitimate technical position, not an 'ai_washed' scam.

This role clearly outlines real AI engineering work, specifying responsibilities like "design and ship agentic and RAG solutions" and "Builds DSPy and LangChain agents, tunes retrieval, and operationalizes GenAI". It also lists a comprehensive and relevant tech stack, including "DSPy, LangChain, Databricks, Hugging Face, PyTorch". There are no scam signals present in the job description.

The job description outlines a clear and specific engineering role: 'building multi-agent systems for field-service automation' and 'Designs and orchestrates agents with AutoGen and CrewAI, integrating LLMs into production workflows.' While the leadlens classifier indicates 'no comp' as a red flag, the detailed technical content of the role prevents it from being classified as an 'ai_washed' scam according to the provided rules, which require a lack of real engineering work in addition to scam signals.

This job description outlines a clear engineering role for a "Senior Backend Engineer" focused on "building AI agents, RAG pipelines, and LLM products" and owning "backend services that run agent orchestration and retrieval at scale." While compensation details are not explicitly mentioned, this does not constitute an "ai_washed" scam, as there are no signals of partnership opportunities, equity-only with no salary, or other deceptive patterns. It describes real technical work.

The job description explicitly details a "Staff-level ML engineer" role focused on "building agentic AI, RAG pipelines, and LLM products" within a cybersecurity platform. It also outlines responsibilities like "Sets technical direction for GenAI features and mentors across teams," indicating genuine engineering and leadership content. There are no signals of a partnership, lack of salary, or marketing/sales focus.

The job description clearly outlines a "Staff engineer" role with specific engineering content, including building "AI agents, RAG pipelines, and LLM products" using a "Stack: agentic frameworks, RAG, LLM APIs, Python." Despite the "unusually wide pay band," there is sufficient technical detail to indicate a genuine engineering position, not an "ai_washed" scam.

This role clearly defines an engineering position focused on building test infrastructure for AI systems, rather than being a sales or marketing role using AI keywords. The description specifies real technical work like "builds test harnesses and evaluation tooling for agentic and LLM features" and lists a concrete stack including "AutoGen, CrewAI, test automation, Python." This indicates a genuine technical contribution.

The job description clearly outlines a real engineering role focused on "building the pipelines and data plumbing that feed LLM and retrieval systems" with specific technologies like "Python, LangChain, LlamaIndex, data pipelines." Although the leadlens classifier flagged "no comp," the presence of concrete engineering work means this does not fit the "ai_washed" criteria requiring *no* real engineering work *and* a scam signal.

The job description clearly outlines a "Software Development Engineer III" role focused on "software engineering and analytics infrastructure" by integrating "AWS Bedrock and Anthropic models". Despite the leadlens flag "no comp," the JD itself does not state "no salary," "partnership opportunity," or other explicit ai_washed scam signals. It describes concrete engineering work, confirming it as a real engineering role.

This job description clearly outlines a real engineering role focusing on "ETL/data engineer for GenAI systems" with responsibilities like "builds the ingestion and transformation pipelines that populate vector stores and feed RAG." It specifies a technical stack including "Pinecone, Agno, ETL pipelines, Python" and explicitly states it is for "data movement and quality," indicating concrete engineering work rather than vague AI marketing or scam patterns.

This job description clearly outlines a backend engineering role focused on "backend service development and API integration" with a defined stack including "MongoDB, AWS, REST APIs, vectorization." It explicitly states the "AI/LLM piece is integration-layer, not model or agent building," indicating a real, adjacent engineering function. Despite the classifier flagging 'no comp', the description itself does not feature any of the typical 'ai_washed' scam patterns like 'partnership opportunity,' 'no salary,' or MLM-style recruitment.

This job description clearly outlines real engineering tasks, including "building FastAPI backends and Next.js frontends" and "integrating multiple LLM APIs (OpenAI, Groq, Gemini) with fallback/retry logic." It specifies concrete technical work and does not exhibit any scam patterns like "partnership opportunity," "no salary," or a lack of engineering content.

This role clearly states it's an "Entry-level/associate software engineer on an AI team" contributing to "LangChain and LangGraph-based features." It explicitly mentions "AI exposure is real but junior and supervised," indicating genuine engineering work rather than a disguised non-engineering role.

The job description explicitly details real AI engineering responsibilities, including "end-to-end development of computer-vision and generative-AI problem statements" and "researches, designs, trains, and deploys scalable ML systems". While the leadlens classifier noted "no comp", the core of the role involves clear, substantive machine learning engineering tasks, not a "partnership opportunity" or a non-technical role disguised as AI.

The job description clearly outlines real AI engineering tasks, including "Trains and optimizes deep-learning models for real-time detection (YOLO v6/v7/v8)" and "Handles model quantization, conversion, and hardware-specific optimization." Despite the absence of compensation details, the explicit technical content confirms this as a genuine engineering role, not an "ai_washed" scam.

This job description clearly outlines a real engineering role focused on "scalable MLOps pipelines - data ingestion, feature engineering, model training, validation, deployment, monitoring, and retraining" and lists specific technologies like "Python, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, AWS/Databricks". Despite the leadlens output noting "no comp", the description itself does not state "no salary" or "equity-only with no salary for first N months", nor does it exhibit any other scam signals. It describes legitimate technical work at a known company.

This job description clearly outlines a real engineering role focused on "end-to-end ML/MLOps infrastructure" using technologies like "PyTorch/PyTorch Lightning/TensorFlow, with geospatial/remote-sensing data." Despite the company being "Undisclosed" and no explicit compensation details, the presence of strong technical content and a detailed stack indicates a genuine engineering position, not an "ai_washed" scam.

This job description clearly outlines a real engineering role focused on "building and fine-tuning language models for a conversational-intelligence product, plus LangChain-based features." It explicitly mentions "model/NLP depth" and a technical stack including "LangChain, NLP, Python, model training," indicating genuine AI engineering work rather than a scam pattern.

This job description outlines a genuine Machine Learning Engineer role focused on computer vision, specifying responsibilities like "Leads CV model development - detection, recognition, segmentation, tracking - and advances an in-house Large Vision Model" and "Builds synthetic-data pipelines, benchmarks, and MLOps automation." It also lists specific technical requirements and a stack. Although compensation details are absent, this omission does not constitute an 'ai_washed' pattern given the substantial engineering content, as it doesn't state "no salary," "partnership opportunity," or "equity-only with vague description."

The job description outlines a legitimate Machine Learning Analyst role focused on "model evaluation, analysis, and applied experimentation" within a known company, EarnIn. Although the leadlens classifier noted "no comp," this indicates a lack of compensation details in the text, not an explicit statement of "no salary" or "equity-only with no salary for N months" as required for an `ai_washed` flag.

This posting uses "AI Partner" in the title but explicitly states it is "pure B2B SaaS sales" with "No engineering." The role is also "commission-only," indicating no fixed salary, which is a common pattern for ai_washed roles that are not genuine engineering jobs.

This posting is for an 'AI Sales Representative' but explicitly states 'Zero technical AI work' and describes it as a '1099, commission-only sales role with full residual... no base.' The 'AI-enabled' framing is a 'marketing veneer' over a purely sales position with no engineering content, fitting the definition of an ai_washed role.

The role's title is 'Specialist Seller, AI Products' which uses AI vocabulary. However, the description clearly states it is 'fundamentally enterprise sales' with duties like 'drives go-to-market execution' and uses tools like 'Salesforce/Clari, not Python or LLMs', indicating no actual AI engineering content.

This job posting is ai_washed because it has 'AI Solutions' in the title, but explicitly states 'no hands-on engineering - no code, models, or pipelines' and describes a 'customer-success/strategy' role. This matches the pattern of using AI vocabulary for a non-engineering role.

The job title includes 'Legal AI', but the description explicitly states "'AI' is the product domain, not the work; this is a business-development leadership role with no engineering content." While the role is non-technical, it describes a legitimate 'Director of Partnerships' position without any indicators of being a scam, such as no salary, equity-only, or 'partnership opportunity' in a deceptive manner.

The posting describes an 'AI Trainer' role focused on 'data-annotation tasks' and explicitly states it is 'hourly gig work with no engineering, design, or system-building.' While it contributes to AI, the role is not an AI engineering position, fitting the pattern of an AI-titled role lacking engineering content and offering gig work instead of a traditional job.

This role describes "Real coding involved" where the contractor "creates and solves programming problems, builds small apps, and synthesizes data analysis". While it is a "contractor role, not FTE" and "piecework data generation" rather than a traditional software engineering position, it involves concrete, technical AI-adjacent work and does not exhibit classic scam patterns like "partnership opportunity" or "equity-only with no salary for first N months" alongside a lack of engineering content.

The job description explicitly states, "No AI, ML, LLM, or agent component anywhere in the role." This is a straightforward backend engineering role that makes no attempt to use AI vocabulary to misrepresent itself, and thus does not fit the definition of "ai_washed." The "undisclosed company" is a general red flag, but not an "ai_washed" scam signal.

The job description explicitly states "Conventional enterprise Java work, no AI/ML." This immediately disqualifies it from being "ai_washed," as it makes no attempt to use AI vocabulary or falsely represent itself as an AI engineering role.

This job description explicitly states "No AI/ML/LLM content" and is titled "Java Software Engineer," clearly indicating it is not an AI role. The description outlines a traditional software engineering position focused on "designing, developing, and maintaining software applications with microservices architecture and the Spring Framework," providing real engineering content without any AI vocabulary to be "washed."

The job title and description explicitly state that this is a "Backend Engineer" role with "No AI/ML/LLM in scope - standard product backend work." It clearly outlines real engineering content like designing microservices and distributed systems with a specific tech stack. This role does not use AI vocabulary falsely, nor does it exhibit any scam patterns like 'partnership opportunity' or 'equity-only and vague'.

The job description explicitly states "no AI/ML, RAG, or LLM component" and focuses on building "scalable data pipelines and cloud data solutions" using "Python, GCP/AWS, PySpark, SQL." This is a clear data engineering role and does not use AI vocabulary, therefore it cannot be "ai_washed."

The job description explicitly states 'General full-stack product engineering with no AI/ML/LLM work' and does not use AI vocabulary in the title or description. Since the definition of 'ai_washed' specifically applies to postings that *use AI vocabulary* but are not real AI engineering roles, this posting does not fit that criterion. It is a non-AI role and therefore cannot be 'ai_washed'.

This job description outlines a legitimate "Forward Deployed Software Engineer" role, detailing specific technical responsibilities such as "End-to-end deployment of working AI agents in production - not a demo" and requiring "Deep familiarity with LLM orchestration frameworks". Although compensation is not mentioned, the posting does not include any explicit scam signals like "partnership opportunity, not a job" or "no salary" found in ai_washed postings. It describes a real engineering position.

This job description outlines clear, real AI engineering tasks such as "Design and implement Agentic AI workflows using LangChain and LangGraph" and "Build LLM-powered APIs with Python." It also specifies concrete technologies like "RAG pipelines over enterprise documents" and "pgvector, Pinecone." Despite the leadlens classifier flagging "no comp," there are no scam-specific signals like "partnership opportunity," "no salary for first N months," or a lack of engineering content.

This role involves real engineering work, specifically to "Build LLM agents using LangChain" and requires knowledge of "Python, LangChain, OpenAI APIs". Although it's an "Unpaid internship" with a very low "Performance-based, ranges INR 5,000 to 15,000/month" stipend, it describes tangible technical responsibilities and is not a 'partnership opportunity' or MLM scheme. The presence of real engineering content means it does not fit the 'ai_washed' criteria, which focuses on a *lack* of real engineering work combined with scam signals.

This role is a legitimate contract position for an LLM Engineer, clearly outlining specific engineering tasks such as "ship a hybrid RAG pipeline with reranking + Ragas evals" and detailing a technical stack including "Python, LangChain, pgvector, Cohere rerank, Modal deploy, Langfuse tracing." While it is a part-time contract role, these details confirm it is a real engineering opportunity with a stated hourly rate, not an "ai_washed" scam.

The role describes a concrete engineering task to "build a custom GPT/Claude integration for our CRM" with a "working Python script" as a deliverable. While it is a fixed-price, project-based role with "no long-term commitment" and a "description very thin," it outlines specific technical work, meaning it is not an "ai_washed" scam despite the lack of detail.

The posting explicitly states "Not a job - it's a partnership opportunity" and "No salary - revenue share." It also asks for "4 partners" with AI expertise in non-engineering roles like "Digital Marketing generalist, Web Dev - Graphic/video & LeadGen" and requires an application via "thumbs-up emoji," clearly indicating multiple scam patterns and no real engineering work.

This posting explicitly states "Equity-only role 1-3%" and "No salary for first 6 months," which are strong indicators of an ai_washed scam, especially when combined with a "Vague on what we're actually building - will explain on a call." The "Telegram-first communication" and reliance on "viral X posts as proof of traction" further align with scam patterns.

This job posting clearly describes a real AI/ML engineering role, focusing on "India's foundational AI model stack" and requiring technical skills like "Python, PyTorch fundamentals, exposure to one of (training/fine-tuning, inference optimization, evals)". It offers a "fixed CTC INR 12-18 LPA", indicating a salaried position rather than an equity-only or partnership scam. The mention of culture concerns is an external note, not a scam signal from the job description itself.

This job description outlines a clear engineering role focused on foundational ML training, specifically mentioning fine-tuning models for Indic languages, PyTorch, distributed training (FSDP, DeepSpeed), and managing GPU clusters. Despite the 'no comp' flag from the classifier, the presence of detailed technical responsibilities indicates a real engineering position, not an 'ai_washed' scam.

This posting has 'AI' in the title ('AI Customer Success Manager') but explicitly states "role involves zero AI engineering" and "No coding required." The responsibilities described, such as helping customers "configure prompts and dashboards," confirm it is a customer success role, not an engineering one, despite the AI branding.

The role is described as a "Founding Engineer" with "Founding equity scaled to scope," which implies equity-heavy compensation. However, it clearly outlines real engineering work to "Build after-action review platform for fire departments" and specifies a technical stack including "FlutterFlow, Firebase, Node, Groq/AssemblyAI transcription, Gemini summarization," indicating a legitimate full-stack product build.

The job description clearly outlines a "Senior Software Engineer" role with real engineering responsibilities like building an "analytics platform processing ad exposure and survey data" and managing "APIs, data pipelines, productionizing analytics workflows." It specifies a strong technical stack including "Kotlin/Java, Python, React, MySQL, DynamoDB, AWS, Kubernetes, LLM orchestration," and offers a clear fixed salary range of "$150k-$175k + bonus/equity." This indicates a legitimate, salaried engineering position with clear technical content.

This posting is an example of an "ai_washed" role because the title includes "AI-first" but the description clearly outlines a Customer Success Manager role, not an AI engineering position. The job involves using AI tools like "Claude to triage tickets, write docs, automate repetitive tasks," which describes a user of AI, not a developer of AI systems. The content lacks any real engineering work, fitting the pattern of AI vocabulary without a genuine AI engineering role.

This role is not ai_washed. The description clearly outlines a technical engineering role involving "distributed systems and ML problems" with a specific tech stack including "ClickHouse, Python, TypeScript, React," and even offers a "Public comp calculator," indicating a legitimate salaried position. The leadlens classifier also identifies it as a 'real_ai_role'.

The job description is for a "Senior Developer (Full-stack)" role with a clear engineering focus on "Build real-time structural design and analysis tools". Crucially, the posting explicitly states "No AI/ML in the role," meaning it does not use AI vocabulary misleadingly. While "Comp not specified" is a potential red flag, the role itself does not fit the "ai_washed" pattern of misrepresenting an AI role or displaying other scam characteristics like "partnership opportunity" or "no salary."

The job description clearly outlines a "Full-Stack Engineer" role focused on building an "open-source platform for background jobs at scale" with a defined tech stack. It explicitly states "Infra that serves AI workloads, not AI itself," indicating a real infrastructure engineering position adjacent to AI. While the compensation details are absent, none of the specific "ai_washed" scam patterns like "partnership opportunity," "no salary," or "equity-only with vague description" are present.

This posting describes a role to "Build AI operating system for enterprise fleet operations" with specific technical details about "Real-world adversarial data: telematics, recalls, invoices" and "Agents execute workflows autonomously." It clearly lists a tech stack (TypeScript, React, NestJS, PostgreSQL, AWS) and offers a competitive salary range with equity, indicating a legitimate engineering position rather than a scam.

This role is clearly a software engineering position focused on compilers and robotics for biology lab robots, explicitly stating "Compiler/robotics, not ML." It offers a competitive salary and equity, and describes real engineering work with a specific tech stack. There are no scam signals like 'partnership opportunity' or 'no salary'.

The role is for a 'Field Engineer' at a company building 'Tabular Foundation Models'. While the description states it's 'not core model building' and involves 'demos, docs, integrations, customer adoption', it requires 'Python, data-science fundamentals, model-training knowledge', indicating real technical work adjacent to AI engineering. The lack of specified compensation is a red flag, but it doesn't meet the criteria for an 'ai_washed' scam as there is clear engineering content and no other scam signals like 'partnership opportunity' or 'equity-only with no salary for first N months'.

This posting describes a real salaried engineering role focused on applying AI to automate design workflows and build AI-assisted design tools, explicitly stating "Comp $150k-$300k + equity" and "Real ML/AI engineering work." It does not present any "partnership opportunity" signals, equity-only compensation, or vague "explain on a call" language that would indicate an ai_washed scam.

This role clearly describes a real AI engineering position focused on "Build computer-vision systems for aquaculture monitoring," including tasks like "Detect sea lice, quantify fish weights, optimize feeding." The description outlines concrete technical work, confirming it's a "CV/ML core role." The absence of compensation details is noted but does not indicate a scam given the strong technical content.

This job description clearly outlines real engineering work: "Build real-time drone detection and tracking" and "Deploy CV/ML on NVIDIA edge devices (Jetson/TensorRT)". Although it's a "Contract, flexible hours" role, it contains specific technical content and does not exhibit any of the defined "ai_washed" scam patterns like no salary, equity-only with vagueness, or partnership-not-job language.

The job description clearly outlines real AI engineering responsibilities, including "Own end-to-end LLM product surfaces" and "Run rigorous evaluations and experiments across prompts, retrieval, and agents." It also specifies a concrete salary range of "$180k-220k," indicating a legitimate, salaried role rather than a partnership or equity-only scam.

This role describes clear engineering work: "Build knowledge graph from web data for go-to-market teams". It mentions significant funding, "Raised $38.5MM from top-tier VCs", and a credible team, indicating a legitimate operation despite being vague on compensation details. Vague compensation without explicit 'no salary' is not a scam signal when there is real engineering work.

This job description clearly outlines a "Research engineer position focused on building AI to simulate the world" and "models and infrastructure for creative tools," indicating real engineering work. The mention of "Full benefits" and global offices also points to a legitimate company, not a scam. While "no comp" was flagged by leadlens, the description does not state "no salary" or "equity-only" in a scammy way.

This job description clearly outlines a real AI engineering role, focusing on building "AI-powered medical imaging solutions using deep learning across MRI, PET, and CT modalities." It further details real-world impact with "Solutions deployed on 1,000+ scanners globally," indicating a tangible product and engineering work, without any scam-pattern language.

This role clearly describes real engineering work, specifically developing a "Rust-based SDK for logging, querying, visualizing multimodal data" for "physical AI including robotics and autonomous systems." Despite the leadlens classifier's 'no comp' red flag, the job description itself does not explicitly state 'no salary' or 'partnership opportunity' in a way that suggests a scam, and the presence of clear technical content indicates a legitimate engineering position.

The job description clearly outlines real engineering work involving "modular probabilistic models for finance and scientific research" and "uncertainty propagation and consistency enforcement." While the leadlens output flags "no comp," the posting itself does not exhibit scam patterns like "partnership opportunity," "no salary," or "equity-only with vague description," which would indicate an ai_washed role.

The job description details a real engineering role focusing on integrations, with a clear tech stack including "Golang backend, custom database engine, server-side HTML rendering." Although it states "Uses AI extensively," this appears to be a tool used within a legitimate engineering context rather than a deceptive AI-washed title for a non-technical role. There are no scam patterns such as "partnership opportunity" or "no salary."

This job description for a "Distributed Data Infrastructure Engineer" clearly outlines a real engineering role focused on "autoscaling systems for cloud database and distributed caching layer on S3" and requires "Go, Kubernetes, distributed systems expertise." It does not use AI vocabulary misleadingly, nor does it present any of the scam patterns such as "partnership opportunity" or "no salary for first N months" with vague descriptions.

The job description outlines clear, real engineering work, stating "Build end-to-end with Next.js and Django including integrations, webhooks, background jobs" and "Uses LLM APIs extensively for healthcare operations." While the leadlens classifier flagged "no comp," the JD itself does not explicitly state "no salary" or "equity-only" in a vague context, and the presence of significant technical detail indicates a legitimate engineering role.

This job description outlines a genuine engineering role involving specific technical tasks like "FMCW/MIMO signal processing to deployed embedded models (ONNX, TensorRT, edge TPUs)" and building "radar + ML for smart cities, sports analytics, defense perception." It specifies working with "Real hardware with real customers including Dutch police and KLM," indicating a concrete product and customer base. Despite the leadlens flag about 'no comp', the JD itself does not contain any language signalling a partnership, no salary, equity-only with vague descriptions, or MLM patterns, which are the core criteria for an 'ai_washed' scam.
